name: Sprint 9

team.name: team-lead
team.owns: arbitration, src/template content, ADRs, final approval

team.name: builder
team.owns: src/bin (writeRecord helper, CLI, channel-guard hook, migrate-records)

team.name: viewer
team.owns: status-page viewer that reads .excn work-tracking JSON

team.name: scribe
team.owns: work-tracking JSON, CONTEXT.md terms, Retro Loop

team.name: packager
team.owns: npm release: src/package.json, versioning, tagging, publishing

goal: Ship PRD-011 conflict-free work-tracking — UUIDv7 self-minted ids, the writeRecord helper + CLI as sole write path for issues/sprints, the default-off channel guard, the migrate-records migration, template stamp, viewer, and PRD/ADR uuid-minting.

shipped.title: EXEC-097: writeRecord helper core, per-record issue schema, and `issue create` CLI
shipped.summary: Stand up the single write path for issues: author a per-record issue schema, widen issue id / prd reference / PRD id patterns to accept legacy EXEC-NNN/PRD-NNN OR UUIDv7, and build writeRecord (mints UUIDv7, rejects caller-supplied id, derives frozen slug, validates with bundled ajv, canonical serialize, atomic write to .excn/issues/<uuid>-<slug>.json). Exposed as `to-execution issue create`.

shipped.title: EXEC-098: `issue update` and partition move through the helper
shipped.summary: Extend helper and CLI with `to-execution issue update`: update any field except the immutable id, including status flips and relocating a per-file record between backlog and sprint-<N>/ partitions (location-as-state). Rejects id changes; reuses canonical serialization and atomic write.

shipped.title: EXEC-099: Sprint authoring through the helper — whole-file write and step_log append
shipped.summary: Teach the helper to write sprint records (kind=sprint): whole-sprint create/update and a step_log append mode. Emit canonical-sentinel form (arrays last, constant sentinel key dead-last) so concurrent appends produce minimal non-conflicting diffs. Exposed as `to-execution sprint ...`.

shipped.title: EXEC-100: Channel guard: deny raw writes to the issues and sprints homes (feature flag, default off)
shipped.summary: PreToolUse channel guard denying raw Write/Edit to .excn/issues/ and .excn/sprints/, redirecting to the to-execution CLI. Modeled on progress-location-guard with a -rules.cjs data module split from decision logic. Adds a hooks-config feature flag, default OFF in the Template; fail-safe exits 0.

shipped.title: EXEC-105: `execution-to-prd` and the grill mint UUIDv7 for new PRDs and ADRs
shipped.summary: Update the execution-to-prd skill and the grill so newly authored PRDs (JSON) and ADRs (markdown) mint a UUIDv7 id instead of the next sequential number. Ships only after EXEC-097's prd/PRD id-pattern widening so a UUID PRD validates and is referenceable.

shipped.title: EXEC-103: Stamp the per-file layout into `src/template` (fresh-Instance path)
shipped.summary: Src-First: land the per-record schema, the issue/sprint CLI, the channel-guard hook + hooks-config flag, and an empty .excn/issues/ layout in src/template so a freshly-stamped Instance starts in the per-file world with no backlog.json. Fresh path does not error on an empty issues directory; package-qa and code-standards pass.

shipped.title: EXEC-104: Viewer reads the per-file issues directory
shipped.summary: Point the status-page viewer at the per-file layout: glob .excn/issues/**/*.json instead of reading backlog.json. Handle an empty issues directory and a missing backlog.json without erroring, and sort sanely when legacy ids and UUIDv7 ids coexist.

shipped.title: EXEC-101: Adopt the CLI in the team's own write-paths (clerk, scribe, gates)
shipped.summary: Move every live team write-path for issues and sprints onto the to-execution CLI/helper: clerk's partition moves and status flips, scribe's sprint open/close, and the gates' step_log verdict appends. Lands before the channel guard is enabled here. Agent-definition edits route through the Retro Loop / alignment gate.

not_shipped.title: EXEC-102: One-time `migrate-records` command: split partitions, retrofit sprints, then enable the guard
not_shipped.summary: Build a one-time `to-execution migrate-records` (distinct from location-only migrate): split backlog.json and each sprint issue-companion into per-file <uuid>-<slug>.json records (grandfathering legacy ids), retrofit every sprint_<N>.json to canonical-sentinel form, all via the guard-safe helper. Verify-before-destroy and idempotent; dogfood run and guard enablement sequenced by the Team Lead.

retrospective_notes.title: Named-constants rule tripped by a pre-existing bare literal surfaced by new code
retrospective_notes.body: EXEC-104's daemon endpoint added a second occurrence of a pre-existing bare 'no-store' literal, which tripped the named-constants gate even though the literal predated the change. Pattern: when new code touches a file carrying a pre-existing bare literal, promote that literal to a named constant as part of the change rather than leaving it for later.

retrospective_notes.title: Adversarial gap-hunt on the breakdown caught schema and lockout hazards pre-code
retrospective_notes.body: The adversarial gap-hunt against the PRD-011 breakdown caught the schema collection-vs-per-record contradiction and the self-lockout hazard (the running team locking itself out via the channel guard before adopting the CLI) before any code was written. High value; it kept its place in the flow.

retrospective_notes.title: Adversarial breakdown review covered read logic but missed the serving layer
retrospective_notes.body: The viewer-server daemon gap — the whitelist could not serve the per-file layout — was caught by viewer during EXEC-104 implementation, not by the adversarial breakdown review. The review covered the viewer's read logic but did not reach the daemon serving layer.

retrospective_notes.title: Trust deployed state over message order resolved stale re-acks
retrospective_notes.body: Teammate message-crossing produced stale re-acks (builder re-acking already-shipped work). The PROCESS rule 'trust deployed state over message order' resolved it cleanly: reading disk showed the true shipped state regardless of message arrival order.
